meta.component: 4 of 4 — Macro / event-stream reader (the 'matchup + tempo' layer)
meta.purpose: Turn the live GEP event stream (kill_feed, death, roster, ult_charge) into three coaching reads: (1) personal nemesis, (2) team-level threat, (3) stagger/tempo. This is the 'help you PLAY' layer none of the competitors occupy — they coach the draft, not the fight.
meta.compliance: Your-side / on-screen data only. Your deaths, your team's deaths, your team's ult_charge. Killer identity comes from the in-game kill feed (public, on-screen) — not a confidential stat. No enemy confidential stats, no ult prediction, no bans.
meta.philosophy: Repositions the product from opponent-scouting toward self-improvement coaching. Safer compliance posture AND a genuinely unoccupied niche.

BUILD_GATING_UNKNOWN.question: Does the GEP `kill_feed` event attribute the KILLER (character_name or a uid you can map via roster) alongside the victim?
BUILD_GATING_UNKNOWN.why_it_matters: Reads #1 (personal nemesis) and #2 (team threat) REQUIRE killer attribution. If kill_feed only reports the victim, those two reads are NOT buildable as designed.
BUILD_GATING_UNKNOWN.fallback_if_no_killer: Read #3 (stagger/tempo) still works — it needs only death timestamps + is_alive + ult_charge, no killer. So even worst case, the tempo layer ships.
BUILD_GATING_UNKNOWN.action: Confirm the kill_feed payload shape FIRST in the GEP Simulator / data spike, before building #1 and #2. This is the single load-bearing unknown for this component.

inputs_from_gep.roster_xx: uid, character_name, is_teammate, is_local, is_alive, ult_charge (teammates only)
inputs_from_gep.not_available: NO player coordinates — positioning is INFERRED from death patterns, never actual location.

fight_segmentation.comment: Several reads operate per fight. Derive fights from death clustering since there's no explicit 'fight' event.
fight_segmentation.heuristic: Deaths within a sliding ~10s window belong to the same fight; a gap > ~12s starts a new fight.
fight_segmentation.status: DEFAULT heuristic — tune against real games; numbers are starting points, not gospel.

1_personal_nemesis.logic: Tally, per enemy character_name, the kills on YOU (is_local victim). Surface when one crosses a threshold within the active window.
1_personal_nemesis.window: Per-round or rolling-recent — NOT cumulative over the whole match. A mid-game fix changes the pattern, so old deaths must decay out.
1_personal_nemesis.threshold_default: >=3 kills by the same enemy hero on you within a round, OR >=50% of your recent deaths from one hero.
1_personal_nemesis.enrich_with: counter_kb — look up that hero's matchup advice so the output is COACHING, not a stat.
1_personal_nemesis.output_template: '{hero} has killed you {n} times this round.' + counter_kb mechanical tip, e.g. 'He's fragile and telegraphed — punish him mid-combo, save your stun for his ult.'
1_personal_nemesis.why_novel: Players FEEL like one flanker keeps getting them but don't know it's 5 of 7. Invisible otherwise; no stats tool surfaces it.

2_team_threat.logic: Tally, per enemy character_name, kills on your WHOLE team (is_teammate side). If one enemy hero is farming multiple teammates / your backline, it's a team problem, not a you-problem.
2_team_threat.threshold_default: >=4 kills on your team by one enemy hero within a round, spread across >=2 different victims.
2_team_threat.cross_ref: comp_gap_model — if the threat's archetype (e.g. dive_flank) matches an uncovered function in YOUR comp (e.g. anti_dive_peel == 0), the read becomes a coverage-gap diagnosis.
2_team_threat.output_template: 'Their {hero} has {n} kills on your backline — your comp has no anti-dive. Someone peel, or swap to {pick from user pool that provides anti_dive_peel}.'
2_team_threat.disambiguation_note: Same raw data as #1 but a DIFFERENT lesson. #1 = your duel/positioning. #2 = team coverage. The split is decided by victim spread + comp_gap cross-ref.

stagger.logic: Within a fight, examine YOUR team's death timestamps. Spread-out deaths = trickling in one at a time (the #1 macro leak below GM).
stagger.signal_default: Median inter-death gap among your team's deaths in a fight > ~4s AND deaths span > ~10s -> staggering.
stagger.output_template: 'You're trickling in — group up before the next engage.'
stagger.caveat: Clustered deaths aren't automatically good (could be a wipe). Tune to distinguish even trade vs trickle.

ult_tempo.logic: From teammate ult_charge + is_alive: detect collective power spike vs ult starvation.
ult_tempo.spike_default: >=3 teammate ults ready (>=95%) AND >=5 alive -> 'Your fight — force it now.'
ult_tempo.starved_default: <=1 ult across the team after a lost fight -> 'Ult-starved — stall and regroup, don't contest this one.'
ult_tempo.note: Pure your-side data. Never references enemy ult state.

first_death.logic: Per fight, record who on your side died FIRST and to which enemy hero. Aggregate over the match.
first_death.output_template: 'You died first in {x}/{y} fights' / 'Their {hero} got the opening pick on you {n} times — respect that angle.'

output_modes.live_glanceable: Short callouts only — nemesis alert, stagger warning, tempo window. Fits the live overlay tier (deterministic, instant).
output_modes.post_game_llm: Full pattern narrative via the LLM layer — 'Spider-Man killed you 5x in rounds 1-2, then you adjusted'; first-death trends; coverage diagnoses. Runs on the slow post-game moment, not mid-fight.

thresholds_note: All numeric thresholds are tunable defaults. Expose them in config; tune against your own games + rank data. Don't ship them as fixed.
caveats: kill_feed killer attribution is UNCONFIRMED — gates reads #1 and #2 (see BUILD_GATING_UNKNOWN).
No coordinates in GEP — all positioning is inferred from death patterns, not real location. Don't claim spatial precision.
Fight segmentation and all thresholds are heuristics to validate, not verified constants.
Keep any tilt/queue-discipline nudges light and non-clinical — a nudge, not a diagnosis.
